item.title: Re: Question I've had for like 6 years
item.content:encoded: It says in the description that lead forces it to enter and exit it's pocket dimension multiple times, also, it says it can be confused by randomly aligned or overly complicated structures.

item.title: How would the 106 mucus react to water?
item.content:encoded: Due to SCP-106 I have been a lot more productive, the lack of sleep has given me a lot more time to do other things. Also, how would the 106 mucus react to water? Would it poison the water, or would the water dissipate the liquid.

item.title: Re: Question I've had for like 6 years
item.content:encoded:
I'd like to point out a couple things for you. First of all, 106 "hunts" when he wants to, so making it harder to even get out might tire him out and make him not want to hunt anymore, at least for a little while.
Secondly, this:

The container will be sealed within forty layers of identical material, each layer separated by no less than 36cm of empty space.

The layers are all separated from each other, so he has to go in and out of his pocket dimension forty times (from what I'm getting he can't just waltz through walls, but has to enter and exit the pocket dimension for it).
Also, the sudden bright lighting might make him retreat back to the "main" chamber and, as said before, could discourage him from hunting for a bit.

item.title: Question I've had for like 6 years
item.content:encoded:
Don't get me wrong, I love 106; it's a classic, and it's always been one of my favorites. But I've always been puzzled by how the containment methods even work. Based on the info in the article, it seems to me like the containment procedures listed shouldn't be effective at all.
For example, it lists that support struts between the 40 layers of lead should be randomly spread, but… why? How would that impede the ability of 106 to just enter his pocket dimension from the inside of the cell and then emerge on the outside of the outermost layer? Similarly, since it can pass through any solid matter at all, what is stopping it from just waltzing through it's cell wall and dropping to the floor below?
I get that the containment methods are designed to slow down or "confuse" it, rather than stop it completely, but I don't really understand how they even accomplish that. If there's never been anything stopping it from just waltzing out of its cell, why would things like organization of support struts and the material composition of the cell employ any reduction in breaches at all

Is there some obvious explanation in a Tale or something, that I've just overlooked for all these years, or am I just looking too much into this

item.title: Re: Original original.
item.content:encoded: Possibly, though I don't really feel like it.

item.title: extremely belated upvote
item.content:encoded:
For some reason I didn't notice until today that I hadn't upvoted this, despite the fact that without this and the other major SCP's featured in Containment Breach (particularly this and 173), I probably wouldn't know about the Foundation at all.
(And besides sentimental reasons, let's face it, this thing is freaking terrifying, preying on the fear of aging/decay/deaths, among others. Despite that, however, Gears has somehow managed to write it in such a way that I believe it's at least "sort of" contained, so I have to give credit for that.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My god, I miss the original but that new pic is absolutely terrifying and gorgeous! I’m almost always biased towards classics, but this does the original justice.

Amazing job whoever worked on this! Sorry if this happened a long time ago, but this is the first I saw of it myself.

item.title: My Review
item.content:encoded: SCP-106 is arguably the best Keter SCP and one of if not the best Humanoids. This kind of work is what defines our wiki. My first pro is the images. What would you do if you saw that thing coming through your door? Die. You are definitely gonna die. This article somehow conveys beyond ANY minuscule percentage of a doubt, that this old man could and would kill you. The femur breaker gave me a fucking nightmare when I first read this. That’s the only time I’ve had an SCP related nightmare ever. Dr Gears is amazing. He really is. And so are the many, many people who fixed errors along the way. This is without a doubt my favorite piece of writing in the world. This is what SCP is. Amazing writers, amazing writings. Keep it up boys.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ohhhh, that's what you meant. Yeah, I'm not sure about that either. I guess since they all stand pretty well in their own it was an "all or nothing" scenario?

They could either link all the stories back to this, or not include them at all? Cause if they didnt include the others, then maybe readers might think that the one included is the true "canon," so they had to choose to either add them all, or ignore them all.

This is just a theory as to why they did that, I'm not to sure about that myself, however other SCP articles have done something similar to this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yes, I'm familiar with the lack of an overarching canon on this site.

I'm saying that there's not really any need for them to be here. Furthermore, they feel shoved in carelessly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
All those stories link back here because all those stories are possible backstories to 106.
Afterall, there is rarely a "canon" in the SCP universe as many SCPs and tales tend to contradict one another. As such, many people make their own tales and it's up to us, the reader, to choose what WE believe is the truth, or at least, make the most sense.

An example or this is with SCP-001. There are multiple variations of it. The gimmick of it is that it's one true article hidden among a bunch of false articles. We dont know what is the "true" article and it's up to urs to deduce and decide which one we think is the true SCP-001.

The EXACT same is applied here. From all those stories, which one of those do you think seems like an origion story to 106?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So it may be just me, but it seems a bit odd to have the Young Man, Once But Not Now, and Until Death all linked in this spot.

It kinda feels a bit weird to have three very conflicting backstories in the doc, and I gotta say that they feel kinda shoved in carelessly. I realize edits wouldn't really be popular, but still…

Not that its changing my vote or anything, just me screaming/loudly speaking into the void.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Does anyone know what these are actual pictures of? I would really like to know.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm loving the new images. I'll miss the old ones, but I won't deny that these new ones are great.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You know what I like about this one?

Both the anomaly itself and the containment procedures. Specifically both when together. This is quite a lot of creativity in procedures derived from an already unique anomaly. Overall, +1.

item.title: Re: Original original.
item.content:encoded:
If there's a way to ensure that the first image is a legal derivative and compliant with the site's creative commons licensing, then it would be simple to switch back.

Can you provide this information?

item.title: Original original.
item.content:encoded: Switch back to the first image.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The pictures don’t look realistic, looks nice just not like the original.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If comments could be upvoted…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: clay figure with drawing overlay

item.title: mid emergence
item.content:encoded:
could be changed to:
"midway through emergence"
or simply
"mid-emergence"
just my 2 cents

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A perfect example of what a monster SCP should look like.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
FOR THE LOVE OF GOD THE PICTURES
The first one took three years from my life, then the third one poped up and OH MY I AM DEAD. It's more than creepy. I shouldn't have read this at night.
That must be what heart attacks feel. Really, that's why I never, never watch horror movies…
Weeeell done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That new photo of SCP-106 is too mediocre, original version was very "creepy" and, but this version looks like a regular zombie from Resident Evil

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Jesus christ that third image with the Agent. That gave me chills.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just heard of the changes to SCP 106. While I will always have nostalgia for the old images I do really like the new ones. The new image of 106 itself looks very ominous and gives off a more decayed appearance. And geez that new third image. I dunno whether that is just a drawing or photoshop but it looks quite disturbing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: GOOOD. NEW JMAGE

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh fuck
The images got updated, now my pants are brown

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: All flex tape and teamspeak jokes aside, I'm surprised how well this is written (Not very surprised as our lord and saviour DrGears wrote it.) This is a complete punch-to-the-face to the opinion that all series-one-humanoids are bad. Take that GMDianaty.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "To show you the power of 106, I cut this tendon in half!"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I just realized that this got a new picture a while back by reading the comments. I will say that the old pictures never truly scared me, but these new photos adds a whole new feel to the article. Wish I could upvote twice.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Flex seal the entire site. I'd like to see him walk through that shit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's not very wacky tbh.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Imagine walking down an empty hallway and this fucker just sliiiiiides out of the damn wall like a source game NPC with buggy collisions and hearing a bass boosted variation of the teamspeak "USER JOINED YOUR CHANNEL" join sound.
Scariest shit i've thought about.
Would like to point out i'm rather high while writing this.

item.title: New picture
item.content:encoded: “You got games on your phone?” -106

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is fuckin sick dude. Thanks for the new band!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The new picture for 106 looks allot better. The last one was good but it was kinda weird considering where the person taking the picture would have been standing.

With the new one is better because it looks like the person had just scrambled out his or her camera and managed to snap a photo of 106. Probably before getting the heck out of there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Can you not have a new picture…

FOR FIVE MINUTES????

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I approve of this new image.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My man Larry has a coat! Nice touch.

That aside, I like 106's new picture. Good job, you guys

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: very big fan of the final replacement

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Nobody knows the original sources of the older images.

I think they mean the updates as the original wouldn't be "fanart"

The door and victim (as well as the unfiltered 3rd revision of 106) can be found at Cinemamind's tumblr. I'm unsure as to the creator of image 2 and 4 though I imagine 2 was taken down from anywhere official as it failed to meet the CC requirements.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think I prefer the current one now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
On a similar note, can I get the links to the original sources of each fan art image and the source of the new one?

Nobody knows the original sources of the older images. This hasn't changed since this morning. It hasn't changed in eight years. It's probably never going to.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's unlikely that the original picture was a legal derivitive in the first place.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What if we find the original maker of the picture and he approves of it being used? Will we revert to the original pictures again?

item.title: Re: Image Attribution 2
item.content:encoded: Finally, a replacement picture that looks badass as hell! Kudos to the artist

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Okay I've been watching the image change a lot in the last few days from one painting to another and none of them looked real nor have the real creepy vibe the original, this current one is damn near perfect. Better than the original in my opinion. My only problem, the smile looks a little dumb, kinda looks like he's biting his lower lip, but it's a minor gripe. This image should be the one that stays I hope. Good job whoever did it. That being said though, the other victim image still looks like fan art rather than an image taken by the foundation.

On a similar note, can I get the links to the original sources of each fan art image and the source of the new one? For archival purposes mainly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this new one quite a lot better than the previous ones. Bravo!

item.title: Re: I like the orginal.
item.content:encoded: It wasn't changed because nobody liked it.

item.title: I like the orginal.
item.content:encoded: I liked the original images because they were so nostalgic and where very uncanny to how the picture was taken(Especially the first emergence)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The new picture is exactly what we needed. Bravo.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Love the new (new) image, the best (new) one so far

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Holy 1600 Batman! Great scip, I'm glad we found a picture we all like.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It’s pretty exciting ngl

item.title: Re: Image Attribution 2
item.content:encoded: Much better. Good work.

item.title: Image Attribution 2
item.content:encoded:
The New New Emergence image is by Klay Abele, aka MrKlay, Licensed CC by SA 3.0.

https://www.artstation.com/artwork/9eBL6Q

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well you might want to post that on the reddit thread, but I think you are going to be disappointed, most people seem to prefer the later versions with the corrosion on the wall and the "vest", and I am guessing that option E is going to be it, at this point.
